raw_consumer_statement:
Citi Card called me on XX/XX/year>, concerning a suspicious transaction on my card by XXXX XXXX in the amount of {$160.00}. I confirmed that this transaction was not done and/or authorized by me. They opened a fraud investigation and temporarily credited by amount to my account. I received a letter dated XX/XX/year>, where they informed me that they closed their investigation and reversed the original amount charging me the {$160.00}. With that letter, Citi provided the documents from XXXX XXXX related to this transaction. In it I saw that the vendor delivered merchandise to a person ( male ) in XXXX, XXXX and that they verified that the billing and shipping addresses were the same. This is, of course, incorrect since my last name and that of the purchaser are different, and I live in XXXX, Fl at the same address for 34 years. 
Today, XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Citi Card to dispute the charge which appeared in XX/XX/year> statement again and I explained all of the above to an agent in the fraud department bringing to their attention all the discrepancies noted above. I complained about their shoddy investigation and demanded that they credit my account with the {$160.00}. I was told that they would re-open the investigation but would not commit to returning my money until they finished their investigation.
